message:
feat(mux): resize queue with superseded-by coalescing and async PTY worker retry

Land the mux-layer half of the resize transaction architecture, adding
a high-throughput resize queue to LocalPane and constraint-aware split
improvements to Tab:

localpane.rs (+787 lines):
- `PendingResize` struct capturing sequence number, TerminalSize,
  PtySize, and enqueue timestamp for telemetry
- `ResizeEnqueueOutcome` returning seq, replaced_seq (for coalescing
  metrics), spawn_worker flag, and queue depth hint
- `ResizeCancellationToken` for cooperative cancellation of in-flight
  resize workers when a newer resize supersedes the current one
- `ResizeQueueState` implementing single-slot coalescing: only the
  latest resize is kept, earlier ones are superseded (O(1) memory)
- `enqueue()` / `dequeue_for_worker()` / `superseded_by()` state
  machine methods with sequence-number monotonicity invariants
- `ResizeApplyMetrics` telemetry struct tracking queue_wait_ms,
  apply_duration_ms, attempt count, and superseded flag
- Async resize worker pattern: spawns on first enqueue, loops
  draining the queue, applies PTY resize with retry and exponential
  backoff, logs metrics per apply cycle, exits when queue is empty
- Integration with existing `LocalPane::resize()` call path:
  enqueues rather than applying inline, preserving existing API
  contract while moving the blocking PTY ioctl off the resize
  critical path

tab.rs (+87 lines):
- Additional floating pane allocation and resize constraint
  improvements complementing the localpane resize queue
- Constraint-aware split sizing that respects minimum cell dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
